title: C1 — Remote duplex: reporter on N-1
brief: Build a full two-way remote in four stages. FIELD: the reporter's RE50B into the Scoopy+ S. LINK: the two AETA codecs over IP. STUDIO RETURN: the Scoop5 S return into the console. CONTROL ROOM: monitor the duplex on the Genelecs. Then feed the codec send from the N-1 bus — the program MINUS the reporter's own return — or she hears herself.
objective: Two-way conversation with no echo in the reporter's ears, and the operator monitoring the exchange.
successMessage: Clean duplex — the reporter hears the studio, not herself, and the control room hears the whole exchange. N-1 understood.
notes: Four-stage duplex on the REAL catalog: field capture (RE50B→Scoopy+ mic in), IP link (codec ip↔ip, AoIP), studio return (Scoop5 out-return→console line 1), control-room monitoring (console monitor L/R→Genelecs). R7 is the star: the console pre-routes its own codec return (in-line-1) onto the N-1 bus (control route-in-line-1-to-out-n1 default ON) → the reporter's voice rides her own send → echo. Fix = drop that route (a true mix-minus). The monitor path adds no new logicChecks (R6 belongs to broadcast studios B-side, not this remote console) — it is pure connection depth. Reporter's headphone return (scoopy out-headphone) is her ears, a terminal, not a required cable.
